content.title: 2023 AHA/ACC Guideline for Chronic Coronary Disease
content.organization: American Heart Association/American College of Cardiology

Recommendations.Recommendation: In patients with chronic coronary disease and pathogenic LDLR variants, high-intensity statin therapy is recommended to reduce LDL-C and cardiovascular events. Class I Level of Evidence A.

Sections.Recommendation-Specific Supportive Text: Familial hypercholesterolemia due to LDLR, APOB, or PCSK9 variants warrants aggressive LDL-C lowering and follow-up lipid testing.

genes.context: LDLR pathogenic variants appear in the lipid management section.
APOB is mentioned with lipid-lowering guidance.

sections.summary: APOE carriers with chronic coronary disease may have residual lipid-related risk. Lipoprotein(a) and LDL-C are relevant biomarkers for risk refinement.
sections.recommendations: It is reasonable to measure lipoprotein(a) once in patients with premature coronary disease or a strong family history. Class 2a Level of Evidence B-NR.

genes.context: APOE is mentioned in biomarker risk stratification.

content.recommendation: PCSK9 inhibitor therapy may be considered when LDL-C remains above threshold despite maximally tolerated statin and ezetimibe treatment. Level of Evidence B-R.

genes.context: PCSK9 is co-mentioned with adjunctive therapy.
